Модули¶

Todo

Background sprites are not used because there is no background rendering implementation. This needs to be fixed

Puzzle game created by REI_F. Features: * Sprites customization * Automatic animation when game over * Optimized for Ubuntu run Last version: 1.0 Released: 05/05/2026

class task5.Startscreen(master, assets)[source]¶

Bases: object

Implementation of the game’s start screen.

__init__(master, assets)[source]¶

A constructor method for initializing the parameters of a class object and creating the startscreen window.

During the work, there are the window name, resolution, and scaling parameters will be set, as well as created and placed interface elements, such as a combo box for selecting the game field resolution and START and FINISH buttons. :param assets: a list of paths to game sprites, required for subsequent transfer to the Game child class :param master: the source of the created window, which will be transferred to the Game master child class

return: Nothing rtype: None

board_size_calc()[source]¶

Calculating the resolution of the game field using the value selected by the user on the start screen. If no value is selected, 4 is automatically set as the lowest value.

rtype: None

start()[source]¶

Destroying the startscreen window, creating the game window, and launching the game. Activated when the START button is clicked

finish()[source]¶

Destroying the startscreen window. Activated when the FINISH button is clicked

class task5.Game(master, blockCnt, winSize, assets)[source]¶

Bases: object

Implementation of the gameboard.

__init__(master, blockCnt, winSize, assets)[source]¶

A constructor method for initializing the parameters of a class object and creating the gameboard window.

During the work, there are the window name, resolution, and scaling parameters will be set, as well as created and placed interface elements, such as a combo box for selecting the game field resolution and START and FINISH buttons. Parameters (were transferred from the Startscreen parent class): master : the source of the created window blocknt: expansion of the gameboard winSize: expansion of the whole game window assets: a list of paths to game sprites, required for subsequent

return: Nothing rtype: None

arr_cell¶

arr_cell - “code-status” list DEFINITION: code-status - block sprite ID, depending on its type (linear/angular) and current rotation

STRUCTURE: [0] - block rt code status, [1] - block code status in Hamiltonian traversal, [2] - block position in H/T

blocksize¶

Calculation of the game field block sprite resolution, depending on the selected field size, the sprites will be scaled

timer_v2()[source]¶

timer_v2 - creating an interface and starting a timer

The interface frame is also divided into two frames: one for the timer and one for the buttons. Local variables: rem_time - The time allocated to the player for solving the puzzle depends on the number of blocks selected on the game board.

update_timer()[source]¶

Updating the timer value and animating the progress bar

if self.rungame is False, it will not start. Every 1000 ms, it updates the timer value by reducing the self.rem_time by one with each iteration

stop_timer()[source]¶

Checking self.PID and self.progress and stopping the progressbar correctly

draw()[source]¶

Layer-by-layer rendering of gameboard blocks and binding the button fucntion after click The structure of the sprite block of the gameboard: Frame object -> Canvas object -> background sprite -> pipe sprite

click(event)[source]¶

Transition function required for correct handling of widget clicks

rotate(x, y)[source]¶

Rotating the pipe sprite in the block after click

block_update(x, y, mode)[source]¶

Block sprite update: removing the previous object and drawing a new sprite in one of the modes Update modes: UPDATE - Drawing a new sprite after interacting with a block HIGHLIGHT - highlighting a sprite after connecting it to another highlighted one ENDGAME - Replacing the block sprite with the “correct” version from the Hamilton traversal, which is needed for animating the field after a time over

highlight()[source]¶

Implementation of lighting for the game field blocks when connected correctly

build_path(index, vis_list, path_list)[source]¶

Converting the coordinates of the vertices of a Hamiltonian graph into blocks of a game board

show()[source]¶

Locking the game board and preparing the puzzle solution animation

animate(step)[source]¶

Animation of the puzzle solution and display of the timeout message

labyrinth()[source]¶

Converting vertex coordinates to specific types of game board blocks. Intermediate function between hamilton_gen() and build_path()

remix()[source]¶

Shuffling the game field blocks in a pseudo-random order

hamilton_gen()[source]¶

Building a Hamilton path as an array of coordinates

quit()[source]¶

Ending the game, stopping the timer and destroying the gameboard window

gameboard_lock()[source]¶

Locking the game board before the puzzle solution animation

win()[source]¶

Ending the game with the game field locked, the timer stopped, and a message about winning

new_game()[source]¶

Ending the current game session, stopping the timer, destroying the game board window, and starting a new game

game_over()[source]¶

A transition function that ends the game when defeated and triggers the defeat scenario

Light 'em up!
